Comments are listed chronologically, showing author, body, and when posted

Comments are append-only — once posted, they cannot be edited or deleted.
Authorization must be enforced correctly

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# TaskBoard — Project Management App

A Next.js 15 fullstack application for managing projects, tasks, and team members. TypeScript + Prisma + PostgreSQL on the server, React 19 + TanStack Query on the client.

## Quick Setup (Docker — Recommended)

```bash
# Clone and enter the repo
git clone <repo-url> && cd taskboard

# Start the app and database
docker-compose up --build

# In a separate terminal, set up the database
docker-compose exec web npm run db:seed

# Run the test suite
docker-compose exec web npm test

# The app is now running at http://localhost:3000
```

## Manual Setup (without Docker)

Requires: Node.js 20+, PostgreSQL 15+

```bash
# Run the setup script (installs deps, sets up DB, configures git hooks)
chmod +x bin/setup
./bin/setup

# Or do it manually:
npm install
git config core.hooksPath .git-hooks
cp .env.example .env   # then edit DATABASE_URL if your local Postgres differs
npx prisma migrate deploy
npx prisma generate
npm run db:seed
npm test
npm run dev
```

## AI Tool Conversation Tracking

**This repository is configured to automatically capture your AI coding tool conversation history with each git commit.** This includes conversations from Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, Continue.dev, Cody, Cline, and Windsurf.

This is part of the Ajackus evaluation process. We evaluate how you collaborate with AI tools — your prompting strategy, how you break down problems, and how you review AI suggestions. The captured conversations help us understand your workflow.

**How it works:**
- A pre-commit git hook runs automatically before each commit
- It copies conversation files from AI tool directories (e.g., `.claude/`, `.cursor/`) into `.ai-conversations/`
- These files are staged and included in your commit
- You don't need to do anything — it happens automatically

**What's captured:** Only AI tool conversation logs stored in the project directory. No system files, browsing history, or anything outside this repository.

**If you prefer a tool that doesn't store local conversations** (like browser-based ChatGPT), the screen recording will capture your interactions instead. No additional action needed from you.

## Seed Data

The seed file creates:
- 5 users across 3 projects with different roles (admin / member / viewer)
- 3 projects with realistic task distributions
- 12 tasks spanning all four statuses (`todo`, `in_progress`, `review`, `done`)

All user passwords are: `password123`

| Email | Role on which project |
|-------|----------------------|
| meera@taskboard.dev | admin on Q3 Launch & Internal Tools, member on Onboarding |
| arjun@taskboard.dev | admin on Onboarding, member on Q3 Launch |
| kavya@example.com | member on Q3 Launch |
| dev@example.com | viewer on Q3 Launch |
| lina@example.com | member on Onboarding |

## Authentication

Register or login to get a JWT token:

```bash
# Login
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"meera@taskboard.dev","password":"password123"}'

# Use the returned token
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://localhost:3000/api/projects
```

## API Endpoints

### Auth
- `POST /api/auth/register` — Create account
- `POST /api/auth/login` — Sign in, get JWT
- `GET /api/users/me` — Current user (authenticated)

### Projects
- `GET /api/projects` — List projects you're a member of (authenticated)
- `POST /api/projects` — Create a project (authenticated; creator becomes admin)
- `GET /api/projects/:id` — Project detail with tasks and members (authenticated)
- `PATCH /api/projects/:id` — Update project (authenticated)
- `DELETE /api/projects/:id` — Delete project (authenticated)

### Tasks
- `GET /api/projects/:id/tasks` — List tasks in a project (authenticated)
- `POST /api/projects/:id/tasks` — Create a task (authenticated)
- `PATCH /api/tasks/:id` — Update a task (authenticated)
- `DELETE /api/tasks/:id` — Delete a task (authenticated)

## Tech Stack

- Node.js 20 (runtime)
- Next.js 15 (App Router) / React 19
- TypeScript 5 (strict mode)
- Prisma 6 + PostgreSQL 16
- TanStack Query 5 (client data)
- Zod 3 (schema validation)
- Tailwind CSS 3
- bcryptjs + jsonwebtoken
- Vitest 2 (testing)

1	generator client {
2	  provider = "prisma-client-js"
3	}
4	
5	datasource db {
6	  provider = "postgresql"
7	  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
8	}
9	
10	enum Role {
11	  admin
12	  member
13	  viewer
14	}
15	
16	enum TaskStatus {
17	  todo
18	  in_progress
19	  review
20	  done
21	}
22	
23	model User {
24	  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
25	  email        String
26	  name         String
27	  passwordHash String   @map("password_hash")
28	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
29	  updatedAt    DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
30	
31	  memberships    Membership[]

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

enum Role {
  admin
  member
  viewer
}

enum TaskStatus {
  todo
  in_progress
  review
  done
}

model User {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  email        String
  name         String
  passwordHash String   @map("password_hash")
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt    DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  memberships    Membership[]
  ownedProjects  Project[]    @relation("ProjectOwner")
  assignedTasks  Task[]       @relation("TaskAssignee")
  createdTasks   Task[]       @relation("TaskCreator")

  @@map("users")
}

model Project {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  name        String
  description String?
  ownerId     String   @map("owner_id")
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  owner       User         @relation("ProjectOwner", fields: [ownerId], references: [id])
  memberships Membership[]
  tasks       Task[]

  @@map("projects")
}

model Membership {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String   @map("user_id")
  projectId String   @map("project_id")
  role      Role     @default(member)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  user    User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([userId, projectId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("memberships")
}

model Task {
  id          String     @id @default(cuid())
  projectId   String     @map("project_id")
  title       String
  description String?
  status      TaskStatus @default(todo)
  assigneeId  String?    @map("assignee_id")
  createdById String     @map("created_by_id")
  position    Int        @default(0)
  createdAt   DateTime   @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime   @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  project   Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  assignee  User?   @relation("TaskAssignee", fields: [assigneeId], references: [id])
  createdBy User    @relation("TaskCreator", fields: [createdById], references: [id])

  @@index([projectId, status])
  @@map("tasks")
}

I've finished mapping the codebase. Here's the action plan.

# Action Plan — Task Comments

## 1. Codebase context (what I'm building on)

| Concern | Pattern in repo | Reference |
|---|---|---|
| ORM / schema | Prisma + Postgres, `@@map` snake_case, cuid PKs | [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma) |
| Auth | JWT bearer → `getCurrentUser` → `getProjectMembership` | [src/lib/auth.ts:11-58](src/lib/auth.ts#L11-L58) |
| Role gate (write) | `canEditTasks(role)` → admin/member only; viewer blocked | [src/lib/auth.ts:55-57](src/lib/auth.ts#L55-L57) |
| Role gate (read) | "any membership" → all three roles | [src/app/api/projects/[id]/tasks/route.ts:20-21](src/app/api/projects/[id]/tasks/route.ts#L20-L21) |
| API shape | `/api/projects/[id]/tasks` for project-scoped list/create, `/api/tasks/[id]` for task-scoped ops | [src/app/api/tasks/[id]/route.ts](src/app/api/tasks/[id]/route.ts) |
| Validation | Zod schemas in `src/schemas/` | [src/schemas/task.ts](src/schemas/task.ts) |
| Client cache | TanStack Query, `queryKey: ["project", id]` invalidation | [src/components/TaskDetail.tsx:31](src/components/TaskDetail.tsx#L31) |
| Modal UI | `TaskDetail` is the natural host for the thread | [src/components/TaskDetail.tsx](src/components/TaskDetail.tsx) |
| Test style | Vitest with `vi.mock("@/lib/prisma")` + helpers in `src/tests/helpers/` | [src/tests/api/projects-tasks-search.test.ts](src/tests/api/projects-tasks-search.test.ts) |

**Authorization mapping (matches the requirement verbatim):**
- Read comments → any membership (admin/member/viewer) — reuse the `getProjectMembership` check, no role gate.
- Post comments → admin or member — reuse `canEditTasks(role)`. Viewer = 403.
- Edit / delete → **no routes at all**. Append-only is enforced by absence of PATCH/DELETE, not by a flag.

---

## 2. Phase 1 — Data model

**File:** [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)

Add a new model after `Task`:

```prisma
model Comment {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  taskId    String   @map("task_id")
  authorId  String   @map("author_id")
  body      String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  task   Task @relation(fields: [taskId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  author User @relation("CommentAuthor", fields: [authorId], references: [id])

  @@index([taskId, createdAt])
  @@map("comments")
}
```

Add back-relations to `User` and `Task`:
- `User`: `comments Comment[] @relation("CommentAuthor")`
- `Task`: `comments Comment[]`

Notable choices:
- **No `updatedAt`** — append-only; absence of the column is documentation.
- `onDelete: Cascade` on `task` so deleting a task cleans its thread; `author` left as default `Restrict` so we don't lose audit trail when a user is deleted (matches the "engagement audit trail" framing in the requirement).
- Composite index `(taskId, createdAt)` covers the chronological list query.

**Migration:** `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_comments` then regenerate the client.

**Seed:** optionally add 2-3 comments per Q3 Launch task so the UI has something to render. Skip if the assessor will judge on an empty thread.

---

## 3. Phase 2 — Validation schema

**New file:** `src/schemas/comment.ts`

```ts
import { z } from "zod";

export const createCommentSchema = z.object({
  body: z.string().trim().min(1).max(5000),
});

export type CreateCommentInput = z.infer<typeof createCommentSchema>;
```

`trim()` + `min(1)` prevents whitespace-only posts. `max(5000)` matches the existing `description` cap on tasks.

---

## 4. Phase 3 — API routes

**New file:** `src/app/api/tasks/[id]/comments/route.ts`

Two handlers, mirroring the existing patterns in [src/app/api/projects/[id]/tasks/route.ts](src/app/api/projects/[id]/tasks/route.ts):

### `GET /api/tasks/:id/comments`

1. `getCurrentUser` → 401
2. Load task to resolve its `projectId` (404 if missing) — needed because comments are addressed by task id but authorization is per-project.
3. `getProjectMembership(user.id, task.projectId)` → 403 if `null`
4. Return `prisma.comment.findMany({ where: { taskId }, include: { author: { select: { id, name, email } } }, orderBy: { createdAt: "asc" } })`

### `POST /api/tasks/:id/comments`

1. `getCurrentUser` → 401
2. Parse with `createCommentSchema` → 400
3. Load task → 404
4. `getProjectMembership` → 403 ("not a member")
5. **`canEditTasks(role)` → 403 ("viewers cannot post comments")** — same gate used for task writes; the requirement aligns the role to existing semantics.
6. `prisma.comment.create({ data: { taskId, authorId: user.id, body }, include: { author: {...} } })` → 201

**No PATCH, no DELETE route** — that is the append-only enforcement. Do **not** add a "comments admin override" — the requirement is explicit that once posted, comments cannot be edited or deleted, by anyone.

**Why task-scoped URL, not project-scoped:** the comment thread is bound to a task; routing by `/api/tasks/:id/comments` keeps the parameter list short and matches the existing `/api/tasks/:id` route grouping. Project-membership lookup is one extra `task.findUnique` — cheap and unavoidable since authorization is per-project.

---

## 5. Phase 4 — Types

**File:** [src/types/index.ts](src/types/index.ts)

```ts
export type ApiComment = {
  id: string;
  taskId: string;
  authorId: string;
  body: string;
  createdAt: string;
  author: ApiUser;
};
```

No edit to `ApiTask` — comments are fetched separately (smaller payload, independent invalidation).

---

## 6. Phase 5 — UI integration

**File:** [src/components/TaskDetail.tsx](src/components/TaskDetail.tsx)

Add a comments section below the existing form, above the action row (the delete/cancel/save buttons). Structure:

1. **Header** — "Comments" + count.
2. **Thread list** — `useQuery({ queryKey: ["task-comments", task.id], queryFn: ... })`. Render each comment as: author name, relative timestamp, body (preserve newlines with `whitespace-pre-wrap`). Empty state: "no comments yet".
3. **Composer** — `<textarea>` + post button. Only render the composer if the current user has `member` or `admin` role on the project. For viewers, show a muted line: "you have read-only access to this project".
   - Determine current user's role by looking at `members` (already passed as a prop) and matching against the logged-in user from `getStoredUser()` in [src/lib/api-client.ts](src/lib/api-client.ts#L13).
4. **Submit** — `useMutation` POSTing to `/api/tasks/:id/comments`; `onSuccess` invalidates `["task-comments", task.id]` and clears the textarea.

UI guards are **convenience only**. The server enforces the rule — never trust the client gate.

Chronology in the UI: render in the order returned (ascending `createdAt`). Newest at the bottom is the standard thread convention and matches the "engagement audit trail" framing.

---

## 7. Phase 6 — Tests

Follow the mock-based pattern in [src/tests/api/projects-tasks-search.test.ts](src/tests/api/projects-tasks-search.test.ts). Add `prisma.comment` and `prisma.task.findUnique` to the mock factory.

**New file:** `src/tests/api/task-comments.test.ts`

Coverage matrix:

| Group | Test |
|---|---|
| Auth (GET + POST) | 401 without bearer; 401 with malformed token; 401 if user no longer exists |
| Membership (GET + POST) | 403 if user is not a member of the parent project |
| Task existence | 404 if task id doesn't exist (don't leak project membership before the task check, or vice versa — pick one and assert it) |
| Read access | viewer / member / admin → 200 on GET |
| Write access | viewer → 403 on POST; member → 201; admin → 201 |
| Validation | empty body → 400; whitespace-only → 400; body > 5000 → 400 |
| Chronology | findMany called with `orderBy: { createdAt: "asc" }` |
| Append-only contract | the route module must NOT export `PATCH` or `DELETE` (regression guard — keeps a future contributor from "fixing" it) |
| Cross-task isolation | findMany WHERE always pins `taskId` to URL param |

**Append-only regression test (worth calling out):**
```ts
import * as route from "@/app/api/tasks/[id]/comments/route";
it("never exports edit/delete handlers (append-only contract)", () => {
  expect((route as Record<string, unknown>).PATCH).toBeUndefined();
  expect((route as Record<string, unknown>).PUT).toBeUndefined();
  expect((route as Record<string, unknown>).DELETE).toBeUndefined();
});
```

---

## 8. Risks & decisions to flag

| Item | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Where to invalidate cache after post | New `["task-comments", taskId]` key, not `["project", projectId]` | Comment activity shouldn't refetch the entire project tree on every post. |
| Show edit/delete to commenters? | **No.** | The requirement is unambiguous: append-only for everyone, no exception for the author. |
| Markdown / mentions? | Out of scope. | Render as plain text with `whitespace-pre-wrap`. |
| Rate-limiting / spam | Not implemented. | No existing rate-limit infrastructure in the repo; flag as a future hardening item rather than inventing one here. |
| Author retained on user deletion | `onDelete: Restrict` (Prisma default) | Audit trail concern from the requirement. If user deletion is needed later, switch to soft-delete on `User` rather than cascading comments away. |
| 404 vs 403 ordering on missing task | Check task existence first → 404; then membership → 403 | Simpler and avoids a per-route stance on info leakage; the rest of the API uses the same order ([src/app/api/tasks/[id]/route.ts:27-50](src/app/api/tasks/[id]/route.ts#L27-L50)). |

1. Schema + migration + regenerate client
2. Zod schema
3. API route + handlers
4. Tests (write alongside the route — they're the spec)
5. Type addition
6. UI integration in `TaskDetail`
7. Manual smoke test: seed → login as each of `meera` (admin), `kavya` (member), `dev` (viewer) on Q3 Launch and verify the composer visibility + 403 on direct POST as viewer.

Ready to start on Phase 1 when you give the go-ahead — or tell me which phase to defer / cut.

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

enum Role {
  admin
  member
  viewer
}

enum TaskStatus {
  todo
  in_progress
  review
  done
}

model User {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  email        String
  name         String
  passwordHash String   @map("password_hash")
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt    DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  memberships    Membership[]
  ownedProjects  Project[]    @relation("ProjectOwner")
  assignedTasks  Task[]       @relation("TaskAssignee")
  createdTasks   Task[]       @relation("TaskCreator")

  @@map("users")
}

model Project {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  name        String
  description String?
  ownerId     String   @map("owner_id")
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  owner       User         @relation("ProjectOwner", fields: [ownerId], references: [id])
  memberships Membership[]
  tasks       Task[]

  @@map("projects")
}

model Membership {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String   @map("user_id")
  projectId String   @map("project_id")
  role      Role     @default(member)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  user    User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([userId, projectId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("memberships")
}

model Task {
  id          String     @id @default(cuid())
  projectId   String     @map("project_id")
  title       String
  description String?
  status      TaskStatus @default(todo)
  assigneeId  String?    @map("assignee_id")
  createdById String     @map("created_by_id")

  updatedAt   DateTime   @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  project   Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  createdBy User    @relation("TaskCreator", fields: [createdById], references: [id])

  @@index([projectId, status])
  @@map("tasks")
}

services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: taskboard
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: taskboard
      POSTGRES_DB: taskboard
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  web:
    build: .
    command: npm run dev
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - node_modules:/app/node_modules
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      - db
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://taskboard:taskboard@db:5432/taskboard?schema=public
      JWT_SECRET: dev-secret-change-me
      NODE_ENV: development

  test:
    build: .
    command: npm test
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - node_modules:/app/node_modules
    depends_on:
      - db
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://taskboard:taskboard@db:5432/taskboard_test?schema=public
      JWT_SECRET: test-secret
      NODE_ENV: test

volumes:
  pgdata:
  node_modules:

content.text:
## Done

**Files added/changed**
- [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma) — `Comment` model + back-relations on `User` and `Task`; index on `(taskId, createdAt)`
- [prisma/migrations/20260513112857_add_comments/migration.sql](prisma/migrations/20260513112857_add_comments/migration.sql) — generated, applied
- [src/schemas/comment.ts](src/schemas/comment.ts) — `createCommentSchema` (trim, 1–5000 chars)
- [src/app/api/tasks/[id]/comments/route.ts](src/app/api/tasks/[id]/comments/route.ts) — `GET` (any member) + `POST` (admin/member only). **No `PATCH`/`DELETE`** — append-only enforced by absence.
- [src/types/index.ts](src/types/index.ts) — `ApiComment` type
- [src/components/TaskComments.tsx](src/components/TaskComments.tsx) — thread + composer; viewers see "read-only access" instead of the textarea
- [src/components/TaskDetail.tsx](src/components/TaskDetail.tsx) — wired in
- [src/tests/api/task-comments.test.ts](src/tests/api/task-comments.test.ts) — 30 tests (auth, role gates, append-only contract, validation, chronology, isolation, anti-impersonation)
- [src/tests/helpers/http.ts](src/tests/helpers/http.ts) — added `newPostRequest`

**Verification**
- `npm run typecheck` — clean
- `npm test` — 64/64 pass (30 new, 34 prior, no regressions)
- Live curl smoke against the dev container — all 7 scenarios match expectations

**Authorization summary as built**
- Read: any project member (admin/member/viewer) — all return 200
- Write: admin or member only via `canEditTasks`; viewer → 403 with the explicit message
- Non-member → 403; unknown task → 404; missing/invalid token → 401
- No edit/delete endpoint exists — including for the comment author. The append-only contract is regression-guarded by a test that asserts the route module never exports `PATCH`/`PUT`/`DELETE`.
